# discord_forum_channel (Resource)

Manages a forum channel (`GUILD_FORUM`) in a Discord guild, including its tags, default reaction, sort order and layout.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "discord_forum_channel" "help" {
  server_id            = "123456789012345678"
  name                 = "help"
  category             = "234567890123456789"
  topic                = "Ask questions and get help."
  default_sort_order   = 0
  default_forum_layout = 1

  available_tags = [
    {
      name      = "Unresolved"
      moderated = false
    },
    {
      name      = "Resolved"
      moderated = true
    },
  ]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Channel name.
- `server_id` (String) Snowflake ID of the guild.

### Optional

- `available_tags` (Attributes List) Tags that can be applied to posts in the forum. Managed by value; Discord assigns the ids. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--available_tags))
- `category` (String) Snowflake ID of the parent category.
- `default_forum_layout` (Number) Default layout (`0` not set, `1` list, `2` gallery).
- `default_reaction_emoji_id` (String) Snowflake ID of the default reaction emoji (custom emoji).
- `default_reaction_emoji_name` (String) Unicode emoji used as the default reaction.
- `default_sort_order` (Number) Default sort order of posts (`0` latest activity, `1` creation date).
- `default_thread_rate_limit_per_user` (Number) Default slowmode (seconds) for new posts in the forum.
- `nsfw` (Boolean) Whether the channel is age-restricted.
- `position` (Number) Sorting position of the channel.
- `sync_perms_with_category` (Boolean) When true, the channel's permission overwrites are synced to its parent category on create/update.
- `topic` (String) Forum guidelines (the channel topic).

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Snowflake ID of the channel.

<a id="nestedatt--available_tags"></a>
### Nested Schema for `available_tags`

Required:

- `name` (String) Tag name.

Optional:

- `emoji_id` (String) Snowflake ID of the tag's custom emoji.
- `emoji_name` (String) Unicode emoji for the tag.
- `moderated` (Boolean) Whether only moderators can apply the tag.

Read-Only:

- `id` (String) Snowflake ID of the tag (assigned by Discord).
